title: agropyron repens var. scabrifolium
intro: Agropyron repens var. scabrifolium, also known as creeping couch, is a type of grass that belongs to the wheat grass family. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting respiratory health. Creeping couch can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
